old_claim.note: Search engines improve ad discovery infrastructure, but real-time digital ad exchanges do not strictly require public search indexing.

new_claim_absent: No direct Digital Advertising Exchanges to Search Engines edge remains because the node is scoped to RTB/programmatic ad markets, which transact ad impressions through exchanges, supply sources, and bidders rather than requiring public search indexing.

source_shape.source_locator: OpenRTB overview defines real-time bidding as transacting media by putting an individual ad impression up for bid in real time through a programmatic auction.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source defines digital ad exchange mechanics around impression auctions and programmatic bidding, not search-engine infrastructure.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Search advertising is one channel, but the RTB exchange scope is implemented through auction protocols among exchanges, supply sources, and bidders without a direct search-engine prerequisite.

ontology_before: Digital Advertising Exchanges depended directly on Search Engines even though the edge note already stated that real-time exchanges do not strictly require public search indexing.
ontology_after: Digital Advertising Exchanges retain e-commerce and API ecosystem context while public search indexing is left as adjacent advertising infrastructure, not a direct prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: removed the direct search_engines prerequisite from digital_advertising_exchanges. Preserved: downstream streaming platform links remain unchanged.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to search-engine advertising auctions only.
A source shows RTB ad exchanges require public web search indexing as an architectural prerequisite.
A separate search_ad_auction_platforms node is introduced and intentionally depends on search engines.
short_reason: Programmatic ad exchanges are auction/API infrastructure, not search-engine infrastructure.
